title: linaria elatine
intro: Linaria elatine, also known as toadflax or butter and eggs, is a flowering plant species within the figwort family that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Linaria elatine include its ability to reduce inflammation, calm coughs, and aid in digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
